title: operator== should not be overloaded on reference types
description: "operator==" should not be overloaded on reference typesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe use of==to compare two objects is expected to do a reference comparison. That is, it is expected to returntrueif and only if they are the same object instance. Overloading the operator to do anything else will inevitably lead to the introduction of bugs by callers.public static bool operator ==(MyType x, MyType y) // Noncompliant: confusing for the caller { // custom implementation }On the other hand, overloading it to do exactly that is pointless; that’s what==does by default.public static bool operator ==(MyType x, MyType y) // Noncompliant: redundant { if (x == null) { return y == null; } return object.ReferenceEquals(x,y); }Excep
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.reasoning: Detects equality operator overloads on reference types which can be problematic due to reference vs value semantics confusion.

should_trigger: public class MyClass { public static bool operator ==(MyClass a, MyClass b) }
should_not_trigger: public struct MyStruct { public static bool operator ==(MyStruct a, MyStruct b) }
